label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this square dance problem. So there are five dancers: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each of them starts with a partner: Alice with Helga, Bob with Jamie, Claire with Ophelia, Dave with Karl, and Eve with Rodrigo. Then there are a series of partner switches, and we need to determine who Claire is dancing with at the end. Hmm, maybe I should track each partner change step by step. Let me write down the initial pairings first: - Alice: Helga - Bob: Jamie - Claire: Ophelia - Dave: Karl - Eve: Rodrigo Now, the first switch is Eve and Bob switching partners. Let me think. So Eve was with Rodrigo, and Bob was with Jamie. So after they switch, Eve will be with Jamie and Bob will be with Rodrigo? Wait, is that right? When two people switch partners, they swap their current partners. So if Eve is with Rodrigo and Bob is with Jamie, after switching, Eve would take Bob's partner (Jamie) and Bob would take Eve's partner (Rodrigo). Yes, that's correct. So after first switch: - Alice: Helga - Bob: Rodrigo - Claire: Ophelia - Dave: Karl - Eve: Jamie Second switch: Dave and Claire switch partners. Dave is currently with Karl, Claire is with Ophelia. So they swap partners. Now Dave will be with Ophelia and Claire will be with Karl. Let's update: - Alice: Helga - Bob: Rodrigo - Claire: Karl
